points.text: Add rate limiting or login throttling per IP/user to block brute‑force attempts, reducing DB load.
Implement CAPTCHA or adaptive challenge after several failed attempts to stop automated attacks.
Review and add appropriate DB indexes on login‑related tables; use connection pooling to lessen lock contention.
Enable READ COMMITTED SNAPSHOT isolation to eliminate shared‑lock blocking on reads.
Add a covering index on users(username) and login_attempts(user_id, ip, attempt_time) to speed lookups.
Keep login transactions short: fetch password hash, compare in app, then commit immediately.
Move failed login counters to a separate table or in‑memory store to avoid hot‑spot row contention.
Implement application‑level rate limiting / IP blocking for brute‑force attempts (outside DB but essential).
Enable rate limiting / throttling on login endpoint per IP or account to curb brute‑force attempts and reduce DB load.
Cache authentication data (e.g., password hash, user status) in an in‑memory store to avoid DB hits for each failed attempt.
Implement exponential back‑off or temporary account lockout after N failed attempts to mitigate contention and attacks.
Separate authentication logic into a stateless service or use a read‑replica to offload primary DB during peaks.
